copy.title: Tigers walk off Rays in ten. Brewers demolish Giants. Knicks-Spurs Finals tip tomorrow night.
sportsOther: Detroit barely held on against Tampa Bay after blowing a lead.
Milwaukee demolished San Francisco in a statement win nobody saw coming.
Colorado escaped Los Angeles by one in a sloppy game neither team wanted.

markets.mood: Tech got split down the middle today — Microsoft cratered 4.2% while Apple jumped 2.9%, leaving the Nasdaq barely green at plus 0.5% and signaling that investors are picking winners instead of buying the whole sector.
markets.whyBullet1: Your 401k's tech holdings just got cheaper if you're in Microsoft, but that 4.2% drop doesn't automatically mean it bounces back — it means your allocation shifted without you touching it.
markets.whyBullet2: Watch earnings whispers and Fed speakers through Friday; a weak jobs report or hawkish central bank comment could trigger another round of tech rotation where growth names keep bleeding.
markets.bringUp: Bitcoin dropped 5.5% today while Dell fell 6.6% — so the AI server play is getting hit harder than crypto, which tells you people are worried about whether those massive chip investments actually pay off.
